title: Decision: viewer must default to high-signal graph
summary: Decision: Kage viewer should not render every code and memory node by default. Dependency paths, node modules, generated graph artifacts, and lockfile noise are hidden unless the user opts into dependency noise; the grap
body:
Decision: Kage viewer should not render every code and memory node by default. Dependency paths, node_modules, generated graph artifacts, and lockfile noise are hidden unless the user opts into dependency noise; the graph layout is recomputed around the visible high-signal subset. Diff proposal memory must filter node_modules and generated paths before writing repo-local packets so useless dependency nodes are not created in the first place.
Evidence: Fixed mcp/viewer/app.js, mcp/viewer/index.html, mcp/viewer/styles.css, and parsePorcelainStatus in mcp/kernel.ts after /tmp/kage-test-project showed node_modules paths in Change memory packets.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp
